body:0002.text: Unlisted Projects: Published but invisible on the homepage; only accessible via a direct URL.

body:0003.text: Draft Projects + Preview Links: Still unpublished, but sharable for testing and feedback.

body:0004.text: Folders: unlisting is a project-only setting. To remove a folder from the homepage and the Participate navigation menu, set the folder to Draft instead (note this also hides the folder and its contents entirely).

body:0005.text: Both methods can be used flexibly depending on whether you want restricted participation or pre‑launch testing.

body:0006.text: When to use preview link or unlisted project?

body:0008.text: Project is published but for a restricted audience.

body:0009.text: Shareable via URL but not publicly visible.

body:0012.text: Great for testing, stakeholder review, and UX validation.

body:0013.text: Can regenerate links to revoke access.

body:0018.text: Unlisted: Removed from platform navigation (e.g. homepage widgets) & search engines. Only accessible via direct link.

body:0019.text: ℹ️ You can re‑list/unlist at any time.

body:0020.text: How to create and use a preview link

body:0022.text: Preview links allow anyone, even non‑registered external stakeholders, to test a draft project.

body:0024.text: In the project back office, click Share (top‑right corner).

body:0026.text: Share this link with colleagues or external partners.

body:0028.text: The preview link tries to recreate the user experience therefore it respects access rights (e.g., user group restrictions or account requirements) and phase status.

body:0029.text: A preview link is only for testing a draft project, so it is not suitable for a durable link such as a printed QR code. To prepare a link in advance (for example, a QR code on a leaflet), use the project's public URL instead: the URL stays the same once the project is published, so a QR code created while the project is still in draft will keep working after you publish.

body:0030.text: Therefore, to make sure people can test the survey, ensure the phase is active: edit the phase dates to make sure it includes the dates of when the testing will happen

body:0031.text: Open to Participation is toggled on (survey only): go to Insights > and toggle on Open for Participation

body:0032.text: There are no phase access restrictions: edit the Phase Access and User Data tab to make sure authentication is set to none

body:0034.text: Click the circular arrow icon next to the preview link to generate a new one.

body:0035.text: Note: Old links immediately become invalid: useful for revoking access.
